description: Remove redundant E2E tests that duplicate coverage already provided by other tests, reducing CI iteration time without losing any unique test coverage.

userStories.title: Delete hot-reload-tools.e2e.ts — fully covered by lifecycle.e2e.ts and hot-reload-dynamic.e2e.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to remove e2e/hot-reload-tools.e2e.ts because every scenario it covers is already tested by other files at the same MCP session level.
acceptanceCriteria: The comment in e2e/hot-reload-dynamic.e2e.ts line 5 that references hot-reload-tools.e2e.ts is updated or removed since the file no longer exists
userStories.notes:
The file e2e/hot-reload-tools.e2e.ts contains 6 tests (239 lines) across 3 describe blocks:

1. 'Hot reload — MCP session tool list' (serial, 3 tests): session survives reload, browser tools callable after reload, 3 rapid reloads don't break session.
2. 'Hot reload — plugin tool dispatch' (serial, 1 test): plugin tool callable after reload.
3. 'Hot reload — tool list consistency' (2 tests): new session sees same tools, health correct after reload.

Every one of these scenarios is already covered by e2e/hot-reload-dynamic.e2e.ts:
- Tool list survival → 'existing session sees correct tool list after hot reload' in hot-reload-dynamic 'Hot reload — plugin installation' and 'Hot reload — multiple MCP sessions'
- Browser tools callable → hot-reload-dynamic tests all call mcpClient.listTools() and verify browser tool names
- Rapid reloads → lifecycle.e2e.ts 'three rapid hot reloads' already tests this exact scenario
- Plugin tool callable → hot-reload-dynamic 'Hot reload — new plugin callable from existing session' tests plugin dispatch after reload
- New session same tools → hot-reload-dynamic 'Hot reload — multiple MCP sessions' covers multi-session
- Health after reload → hot-reload-dynamic 'Hot reload — health endpoint consistency' tests this exact thing

Also update the JSDoc comment in e2e/hot-reload-dynamic.e2e.ts (line 5) which says 'These tests go beyond the existing hot-reload-tools.e2e.ts' — remove or rewrite that reference since the file will no longer exist.

userStories.title: Remove redundant plugin-logging tests 2-4 from plugin-logging.e2e.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to remove tests 2-4 from plugin-logging.e2e.ts because test 1 already validates the full logging pipeline including all 4 log levels and the correct plugin name tag.
acceptanceCriteria: The 3 tests 'log buffer count increases after log_levels tool call', 'log entries include correct plugin name in logger field', and 'multiple log_levels invocations accumulate in buffer' are removed from e2e/plugin-logging.e2e.ts
Test 1 'log_levels tool emits log entries that arrive at MCP server' is preserved unchanged
The imports are updated: remove 'fetchHealth' from fixtures import, remove 'waitFor' from helpers import (only keep setupToolTest, callToolExpectSuccess, waitForLog)
userStories.notes:
File: e2e/plugin-logging.e2e.ts (currently 148 lines, 4 tests in one describe block)

Keep test 1 (lines 16-48): 'log_levels tool emits log entries that arrive at MCP server' — this already validates:
- All 4 log levels (debug, info, warning, error) arrive at the MCP server
- The [plugin:e2e-test] tag is present in logs
- Log level tags (DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR) appear in output

Remove test 2 (lines 50-85): 'log buffer count increases after log_levels tool call' — just verifies logBufferSize increments, which is a trivial counter. The health endpoint's logBufferSize field is already tested in health-endpoint.e2e.ts.

Remove test 3 (lines 87-109): 'log entries include correct plugin name in logger field' — test 1 already asserts `allLogs.toContain('[plugin:e2e-test]')`. This test does the exact same check with a different filtering approach.

Remove test 4 (lines 111-147): 'multiple log_levels invocations accumulate in buffer' — just calls the same tool twice and verifies buffer >= 8. This tests that a circular buffer appends, which is trivial.

After removal, update imports on line 12-13:
- Line 12: change `import { test, expect, fetchHealth } from './fixtures.js';` to `import { test, expect } from './fixtures.js';`
- Line 13: change `import { setupToolTest, callToolExpectSuccess, waitForLog, waitFor } from './helpers.js';` to `import { setupToolTest, callToolExpectSuccess, waitForLog } from './helpers.js';`

userStories.title: Delete build-artifacts.e2e.ts — tests build tooling output, not platform runtime behavior
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to remove e2e/build-artifacts.e2e.ts because it only validates that Bun's build output includes source maps, which is build tooling behavior rather than platform runtime behavior.
userStories.notes:
File: e2e/build-artifacts.e2e.ts (68 lines, 3 tests)

All 3 tests read static files from the e2e-test plugin's dist/ directory and validate:
1. adapter.iife.js.map exists and has size > 0
2. adapter.iife.js contains '//# debugId=' comment
3. Source map is valid JSON with version 3, sources contain 'src/', sourcesContent present

These tests validate Bun's build output format, not any OpenTabs platform runtime behavior. The source map is consumed by browser DevTools, not by the extension or MCP server. If Bun ever changes its source map output format, the appropriate place to catch that is the plugin-tools build command's own unit tests, not E2E tests that spin up a full browser + server infrastructure.

No other test files import from or reference build-artifacts.e2e.ts.
